claim: FAO Food Loss Index estimates 30-40 percent of Sub-Saharan African food is lost postharvest, concentrated at handling, storage, and transport stages, with cereals and perishable crops most affected.

source.title: FAO Food Loss and Waste Database

method: Reviewed FAO Food Loss Index methodology, regional estimates, and commodity breakdowns.
limitations: Food-loss data is sparse and modeled for most countries.
Sub-national estimates are not available for most countries.

claim: APHLIS provides postharvest loss estimates for major cereals at provincial scale across SSA, with losses of 10-30 percent by crop and location.
method: Reviewed APHLIS methodology, loss estimates, and country coverage.
limitations: APHLIS covers cereals only, not perishable crops.
Estimates are modeled from available data, not directly measured.
